label: Evagrius Ponticus, Treatise to the Monk Eulogius and On the Thoughts

work_wit.alt_title: Evagrius Ponticus, Treatise to the Monk Eulogius

excerpt.as_written: ... ܐܝܢܐ ܕܠܟ ܡܢ ܣܓܝ̈ܐܐ ܡܬܩܪܒ ܬܫܒܘܚܬܐ ܗܟܝܠ ܡܢ ܒܣܪܐ ܠܐ ܬܒܥܐ
note: = Gr. Δόξαν ἀπὸ σαρκὸς μὴ ζήτει (ed. Fogielman, p. 280, ch. 3, line 4)

excerpt.as_written: ܟܕ ܐܣܟܝܡܐ ܠܒ̇ܫ ܐܢܬ ܕܡܝܬܪܘܬܐ ܣܘܥܪ̈ܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܡܬܥܛܦ ܐܢܬ ܕܕܠܩܘܒܠܐ ...
ܫܠܡ ܡܐܡܪܐ ܕܥܠ ܐܟܣܢܝܘܬܐ ܕܡܪܝ ܐܘܓܪܝܣ

note: The text is divided in unnumbered sections.

Contents: [lacuna of three folios, one of which is Sinai Syriac NF Sparagma 43], Ch. 3 (f. 1r–v, acephalous), Ch. 4 (ff. 1v–3v), Ch. 5 (ff. 3v–4r), Ch. 6 (ff. 4r–5v), Ch. 7 (ff. 5v–6bis), Ch. 8 (ff. 6bis–7r), Ch. 9 (f. 7r–v), Ch. 10 (ff. 7v–8v), Ch. 11 (ff. 8v–10r), Ch. 12 (ff. 10r–11r), Ch. 13 (ff. 11r–12r), Ch. 14 (ff. 12r–13r), Ch. 15 (ff. 13r–14v), Ch. 16 (ff. 14v–15v), Ch. 17 (ff. 15v–17r), Ch. 18 (ff. 17r–18v), Ch. 19 (ff. 18v–19v), Ch. 20 (ff. 19v–21r), Ch. 21 (f. 21r–v), Ch. 22 (ff. 21v–23r), Ch. 23 (f. 23r–v), Ch. 27 (f. 23v, incomplete), [lacuna of three folios, two of which are BnF syr. 378, ff.  19–20], Ch. 25 (f. 24r–v, acephalous), Ch. 26 (ff. 24v–25v), Ch. 28 (ff. 25v–26r), Ch. 29 (ff. 26r–27v), Ch. 30 (ff. 27v–28v), Ch. 31 (ff. 28v–30r), Ch. 32 (ff. 30r–32r)

bib.citation: Fogielman, Charles-Antoine, ed. Evagrius Ponticus. A Euloge: Les vices opposés aux vertus (Sources chrétiennes, 591). Paris: Éditions du Cerf, 2017

work_wit.alt_title: Evagrius Ponticus, De malignis cogitationibus / On the Thoughts

excerpt.as_written: ܬܘܒ ܪ̈ܝܫܐ ܕܡܪܝ ܐܘܓܪܝܣ ܐܡܝܪ̈ܝܢ ܥܠ ܚܘ̈ܫܒܐ ܕܥܪܨܝܢ ܥܠ ܝܚܝ̈ܕܝܐ ܘܥܠ ܟܠ ܕܒܥܢܘܝܘܬܐ ܡܬܕܒܪ
translation: Chapters by Mār Evagrius about thoughts that occur to solitaries and to everybody who pursues the monastic way of life

excerpt.as_written: ... ܠܘܩܕܡ ܥܠ ܚܘ̈ܫܒܐ ܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܥܒܝܕܝܢ ܣܩܘܒܠܐ ܠܟܠܗ ܕܘܒܪܐ ܕܕܚܠܬ ܐܠܗܐ
ܘܢܚܒܠ ܚܘܫܒܘܗܝ ܘܗܕܐ ܐܝܬܝܗ̇ ܗܝ ܕܟܬܝܒܐ ܕܪܓܙܘ ܘܠܐ ܬܚܛܘܢ ܘܡܛܠܬܗ̇ ܕܗܕܐ ...
note: = Greek, ch. 16, καὶ διαφθείροντα αὐτοῦ τὰ νοήματα· καὶ τοῦτό ἐστι τὸ “ὀργίζεσθε, καὶ μὴ ἁμαρτάνετε” (ed. Guillaumont et al., p. 206–205, lines 19–21)
The Syriac version of the work presents the text divided into chapters, each bearing its individual title and number. Some of the chapters have additional subdivision marked by rubrics.

Contents: Ch. 1 (f. 32r–v), Ch. 2 (ff. 32v–33r), Ch. 3 (ff. 33r–34r), Ch. 4 (ff. 34r–35r), Ch. 5 (ff. 35r–36r), Ch. 6 (f. 36r–v), Ch. 7 (ff. 36v–37r), Ch. 8 (ff. 37r–38r), Ch. 9 (ff. 38r–39r), Ch. 10 (f. 39r–v), Ch. 11 (ff. 39v–40v), Ch. 12 (ff. 40v–41v), Ch. 13 (ff. 41v–42r), Ch. 14 (ff. 42r–43r), Ch. 15 (ff. 43r–v, incomplete), [lacuna of four folios which are today BnF syr. 378, ff. 1–4]

bib.citation: Guillaumont, Antoine, Claire Guillaumont, and Paul Géhin, eds. Évagre le Pontique. Sur les pensées (SC, 438). Paris: Éditions du Cerf, 1998
note: Greek edition of the text|~|On p. 307–309 one can find a concordance between the Greek and Syriac versions; the version attested by the Sinai Syriac 60 demonstrates some differences
